evals.prompt: Add an HTTP REST connector to my process that fetches user data from https://api.example.com/users/{userId}. The userId comes from a process variable.
evals.expected_output: BPMN with HTTP connector applied via c8 element-template apply, configured with GET method and dynamic URL
expectations: Discovers the HTTP/REST template via c8 element-template search (does not guess the template ID from memory)
Uses c8 element-template apply with the HTTP REST template (e.g., io.camunda.connectors.HttpJson.v2)
Targets the process by file path and uses --in-place (or equivalent) to modify the BPMN
Configures method=GET and a FEEL expression for the URL (with the userId variable)
Sets resultVariable or resultExpression for the response
Uses proper XML encoding for FEEL expressions in attributes (when manual edits are made)

evals.prompt: What connectors are available for sending messages or notifications?
evals.expected_output: List of messaging-related connectors discovered via c8 element-template search
expectations: Uses c8 element-template search to discover relevant templates (e.g., search for 'slack', 'email', 'kafka', etc.)
Identifies messaging connectors like Slack, SendGrid, email, RabbitMQ, Kafka
Provides brief description of what each connector does

evals.prompt: I have a service task Task_SendSlack in my process. Configure it to send a Slack message to the #alerts channel with the incident details.
evals.expected_output: Applies Slack connector template via c8 element-template apply and configures channel and message properties
expectations: Discovers the Slack template via c8 element-template search 'slack' (does not guess the template ID from memory)
Uses c8 element-template apply to apply the discovered Slack outbound connector template
Inspects template properties (e.g., via c8 element-template list-properties) to understand required properties
Configures channel and message input mappings
Uses secrets reference for the Slack OAuth token (e.g., {{secrets.SLACK_OAUTH_TOKEN}})
